what_it_does: A standalone executable acceptance test for the ops/cli model-capabilities catalog. It loads the service registry and the capabilities module, then runs five groups of checks: catalog width, capability discrimination, grounded tags, data-driven file reload, and fail-loud loader behavior. It exits 0 only if all checks pass.
observations: Uses sys.path.insert to import bare modules from ops/cli (registry, capabilities).
Implements its own minimal pass/fail harness via check() and global counters.
Hardcodes the resident model id cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ-4bit.
Reads every model-id-bearing services.json entry and asserts it is cataloged.
Queries cap.suitable_models, cap.role_can_bind, cap.provides_for, and cap.capabilities_for.
Injects a new model row into a temporary copy of ops/model_capabilities.json and calls cap.reload_catalog to prove data-driven updates.
Restores the live catalog from the canonical file in a finally block.
Tests that cap._load_catalog raises RuntimeError for missing, malformed, and empty catalogs.
standouts: Explicitly positions itself as the C2.5 catalog lane, distinct from model_capabilities_acceptance.py.
Uses temp-file mutation plus reload as a literal proof of the data-driven bar.
Enforces registry-is-truth: every services.json model-id must appear in the catalog.
Fail-loud loader checks are treated as first-class acceptance criteria.
conventions: Shebang and docstring-heavy script intended to run directly.
Global mutable state for pass/fail counters.
tempfile + try/finally for isolated mutation and cleanup.
Extensive inline comments that double as a specification.
concerns: Hardcoded population assumptions (>=2 embedders, >=3 chatters, >=2 rerankers, >=1 tts, >=2 vision) make the test brittle if the catalog changes.
Directly calls the private cap._load_catalog function.
Mutates global module state (cap.MODEL_CAPABILITIES), which could interfere with concurrent test execution.
Relies on registry content and capabilities schema not visible in this file.
notes: A self-contained acceptance spec that treats the model catalog as file-discovered, fail-loud, and wide enough to cover every declared services.json model id.
questions: What is the exact schema of ops/model_capabilities.json and the capabilities module API?
How does registry.load() locate and parse services.json?
What is the full controlled vocabulary in cap.CAPABILITY_TAGS?
Is this test run in CI, or only manually via the shebang/PYTHONPATH command in the docstring?
What is the precise relationship between this file and model_capabilities_acceptance.py beyond the comment?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it verifies catalog width, capability discrimination, grounded tags, data-driven updates, and fail-loud loading.
intention_signals: To lock in a widened, file-discovered model catalog and prove it can be extended by editing data rather than code, while ensuring missing or bad catalog data raises immediately.
novelty: A standalone, framework-free acceptance script that explicitly claims a separate lane (C2.5) from another acceptance test and uses live temp-file mutation to demonstrate the data-driven criterion.
contribution: A runnable, file-local acceptance check that documents and validates the model catalog's declared width, capability-query semantics, data-driven reload behavior, and fail-loud loader contract.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone acceptance test for the ops/cli model capabilities catalog that verifies full declared-model width, capability discrimination, grounded tags, data-driven file reload, and fail-loud loading.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the C2.5 catalog-lane acceptance spec proving the catalog is file-discovered, covers all declared model ids, and fails loudly on missing or malformed data.

symbols.does: Minimal pass/fail assertion helper that updates global counters and prints PASS or FAIL.
symbols.description: The check function takes a name, boolean condition, and optional detail string. If the condition is true it increments _passed and prints a PASS line; otherwise it increments _failed and prints a FAIL line. It is the only assertion primitive used by the acceptance script.

symbols.does: Runs the full catalog acceptance suite in five sections and exits with status 1 on failure.
symbols.description: The main function loads the registry, runs checks for catalog width, capability discrimination, grounded tags, data-driven file reload, and fail-loud loader behavior, mutates and restores the capabilities catalog via a temporary file, and finally prints the pass/fail tally and exits accordingly.
